briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — The collapse of fintech middleware company Synapse left thousands of Americans unable to access their savings, highlighting systemic risks in the banking-as-a-service model.

- **[A fresh financial crisis may be coming - it won't play out like the last one](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cp3p5l0nyevo?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — Multiple economic warning signs are prompting analysts to question whether the global financial system is approaching another crisis, though its form may differ significantly from 2008.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's deeper ambitions in financial data and credit services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A detailed analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competitive dynamics in the payments industry globally.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Koho users can now buy, hold, and manage crypto](https://betakit.com/koho-users-can-now-buy-hold-and-manage-crypto/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian fintech KOHO has expanded its platform to include cryptocurrency purchasing, holding, and management, deepening its embedded finance offering for everyday Canadians.

- **[AI strategy pillars, new SMB procurement program revealed in Canada's Spring Economic Update](https://betakit.com/canadian-spring-economic-update-2026/)** — *BetaKit* — The federal government's Spring Economic Update unveiled key pillars of Canada's national AI strategy alongside a new procurement program aimed at supporting small and medium-sized businesses.

- **[7 key takeaways from the Liberal government's spring economic snapshot](https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/takeaways-from-spring-economic-update-carney-9.7180305?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — CBC breaks down the Carney government's spring fiscal update, outlining major spending priorities and economic policy signals relevant to Canada's tech and financial sectors.

- **[Canada is getting a sovereign wealth fund. What are they and how might this one work?](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/canada-sovereign-wealth-fund-explainer-9.7179217?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada has announced plans to establish a sovereign wealth fund, with CBC exploring how such a vehicle could be structured and what it might mean for long-term national investment strategy.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — *(Included for ongoing relevance to Canadian open banking policy)* Canada's federal government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and outlined early targets for implementing a formal open banking framework — a regulatory journey still unfolding today.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[The Canada-to-Caribbean startup pipeline is real](https://betakit.com/the-canada-to-caribbean-startup-pipeline-is-real/)** — *BetaKit* — A growing pathway of Canadian startups expanding into Caribbean markets offers relevant lessons for Atlantic Canadian founders eyeing international growth from a coastal, regionally-rooted base.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is disrupting traditional credit card models with novel rewards, underwriting, and embedded finance features — trends with direct implications for Atlantic Canadian credit unions and financial institutions looking to modernize.
